rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical thiol-ene type hydrothiolation/coupling of styrenes with arylthiols mediated by PhB(OH)2 proceeding through thiyl radical addition to the alkene.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of arylthiolate/thiol to generate an aryl thiyl radical (ArS•) that adds to the styrene; PhB(OH)2 acts as a hydroxyl/proton shuttle generating a HO-B(Ph)(NMe2)-type byproduct.

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbon radical from styrene + ArS• addition

electron_flow: At the anode, ArSH (or ArS-) is oxidized to a thiyl radical ArS•, which adds to the styrene to form a benzylic C-radical; in the productive cycle this radical is reduced/protonated to give the hydrothiolation product, while in the TEMPO probe experiment it is trapped as adduct 3n'. PhB(OH)2 mediates proton/hydroxyl transfer, ending up as the aminoborinic byproduct IV detected by MS.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3n suppressed (0% yield); TEMPO-trapped adduct 3n' (C24H33NOS, [M+H]+ calcd 384.2356, found 384.2367) detected by ESI-HRMS, confirming a benzylic C-radical intermediate after thiyl radical addition.

probe_reagents.name: PhB(OH)2 (byproduct capture)
probe_reagents.observation: Putative byproduct IV (HO-B(Ph)NMe2, exact mass 149.1012; [M+H]+ 150.1085 found 150.1046) detected by EI-MS and ESI-HRMS, supporting involvement of the boronic acid as a hydroxyl/amine shuttle.

notes: Only the radical/byproduct trapping portion of the SI was provided; full catalytic cycle and ordering of SET steps are inferred from the TEMPO suppression (radical pathway) and MS identification of the boron-containing byproduct IV. No KIE, CV, or DFT data shown on this page.
